verses.latin_vulgate: Ingressus ergo Joseph nuntiavit Pharaoni, dicens : Pater meus et fratres, oves eorum et armenta, et cuncta quæ possident, venerunt de terra Chanaan : et ecce consistunt in terra Gessen.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֣א יוֹסֵף֮ וַיַּגֵּ֣ד לְפַרְעֹה֒ וַיֹּ֗אמֶר אָבִ֨י וְאַחַ֜י וְצֹאנָ֤ם וּבְקָרָם֙ וְכׇל־אֲשֶׁ֣ר לָהֶ֔ם בָּ֖אוּ מֵאֶ֣רֶץ כְּנָ֑עַן וְהִנָּ֖ם בְּאֶ֥רֶץ גֹּֽשֶׁן׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then Joseph went in and told Pharao, saying: My father and brethren, their sheep and their herds, and all that they possess, are come out of the land of Chanaan: and behold they stay in the land of Gessen.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Joseph tuli ja ilmoitti Pharaolle, ja sanoi: minun isäni ja minun veljeni, heidän karjansa, pienet ja suuret, ja kaikki, mitä heillä on, ovat tulleet Kanaanin maalta; ja katso, he ovat Gosenin maalla.
verses.king_james: Then Joseph came and told Pharaoh, and said, My father and my brethren, and their flocks, and their herds, and all that they have, are come out of the land of Canaan; and, behold, they are in the land of Goshen.

verses.latin_vulgate: Extremos quoque fratrum suorum quinque viros constituit coram rege :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמִקְצֵ֣ה אֶחָ֔יו לָקַ֖ח חֲמִשָּׁ֣ה אֲנָשִׁ֑ים וַיַּצִּגֵ֖ם לִפְנֵ֥י פַרְעֹֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Five men also, the last of his brethren, he presented before the king:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän otti viisi nuorimmista veljistänsä, ja asetti Pharaon eteen.
verses.king_james: And he took some of his brethren, even five men, and presented them unto Pharaoh.

verses.latin_vulgate: quos ille interrogavit : Quid habetis operis ? Responderunt : Pastores ovium sumus servi tui, et nos et patres nostri.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֧אמֶר פַּרְעֹ֛ה אֶל־אֶחָ֖יו מַה־מַּעֲשֵׂיכֶ֑ם וַיֹּאמְר֣וּ אֶל־פַּרְעֹ֗ה רֹעֵ֥ה צֹאן֙ עֲבָדֶ֔יךָ גַּם־אֲנַ֖חְנוּ גַּם־אֲבוֹתֵֽינוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he asked them: What is your occupation? They answered: We, thy servants, are shepherds, both we and our fathers.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Pharao sanoi hänen veljillensä: mikä teidän virkanne on? He vastasivat: sinun palvelias ovat paimenet, sekä me että meidän isämme.
verses.king_james: And Pharaoh said unto his brethren, What is your occupation? And they said unto Pharaoh, Thy servants are shepherds, both we, and also our fathers.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ad peregrinandum in terra tua venimus : quoniam non est herba gregibus servorum tuorum, ingravescente fame in terra Chanaan : petimusque ut esse nos jubeas servos tuos in terra Gessen.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּאמְר֣וּ אֶל־פַּרְעֹ֗ה לָג֣וּר בָּאָ֘רֶץ֮ בָּ֒אנוּ֒ כִּי־אֵ֣ין מִרְעֶ֗ה לַצֹּאן֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר לַעֲבָדֶ֔יךָ כִּֽי־כָבֵ֥ד הָרָעָ֖ב בְּאֶ֣רֶץ כְּנָ֑עַן וְעַתָּ֛ה יֵֽשְׁבוּ־נָ֥א עֲבָדֶ֖יךָ בְּאֶ֥רֶץ גֹּֽשֶׁן׃
verses.douay_rheims: We are come to sojourn in thy land, because there is no grass for the flocks of thy servants, the famine being very grievous in the land of Chanaan: and we pray thee to give orders that we thy servants may be in the land of Gessen.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sanoivat vielä Pharaolle: me olemme tulleet asumaan teidän kanssanne tälle maalle; sillä sinun palvelioillas ei ole laidunta karjallensa, niin kova nälkä on Kanaanin maalla. Niin suo sinun palvelias asua Gosenin maalla.
verses.king_james: They said moreover unto Pharaoh, For to sojourn in the land are we come; for thy servants have no pasture for their flocks; for the famine is sore in the land of Canaan: now therefore, we pray thee, let thy servants dwell in the land of Goshen.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit itaque rex ad Joseph : Pater tuus et fratres tui venerunt ad te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר פַּרְעֹ֔ה אֶל־יוֹסֵ֖ף לֵאמֹ֑ר אָבִ֥יךָ וְאַחֶ֖יךָ בָּ֥אוּ אֵלֶֽיךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The king therefore said to Joseph: Thy father and thy brethren are come to thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Pharao puhui Josephille, sanoen: sinun isäs ja veljes ovat tulleet sinun tykös.
verses.king_james: And Pharaoh spake unto Joseph, saying, Thy father and thy brethren are come unto thee:

verses.latin_vulgate: Terra Ægypti in conspectu tuo est : in optimo loco fac eos habitare, et trade eis terram Gessen. Quod si nosti in eis esse viros industrios, constitue illos magistros pecorum meorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶ֤רֶץ מִצְרַ֙יִם֙ לְפָנֶ֣יךָ הִ֔וא בְּמֵיטַ֣ב הָאָ֔רֶץ הוֹשֵׁ֥ב אֶת־אָבִ֖יךָ וְאֶת־אַחֶ֑יךָ יֵשְׁבוּ֙ בְּאֶ֣רֶץ גֹּ֔שֶׁן וְאִם־יָדַ֗עְתָּ וְיֶשׁ־בָּם֙ אַנְשֵׁי־חַ֔יִל וְשַׂמְתָּ֛ם שָׂרֵ֥י מִקְנֶ֖ה עַל־אֲשֶׁר־לִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: The land of Egypt is before thee: and make them dwell in the best place, and give them the land of Gessen. And if thou knowest that there are industrious men among them, make them rulers over my cattle.
verses.finnish_biblia: Egyptin maa on altis sinun edessäs; aseta isäs asumaan ja veljes kaikkein parhaasen paikkaan tässä maassa: asukaan Gosenin maalla. Ja jos sinä ymmärrät, että heidän seassansa on kelvollisia miehiä, niin aseta heitä minun karjani päälle.
verses.king_james: The land of Egypt is before thee; in the best of the land make thy father and brethren to dwell; in the land of Goshen let them dwell: and if thou knowest any men of activity among them, then make them rulers over my cattle.

verses.latin_vulgate: Post hæc introduxit Joseph patrem suum ad regem, et statuit eum coram eo : qui benedicens illi,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֵ֤א יוֹסֵף֙ אֶת־יַֽעֲקֹ֣ב אָבִ֔יו וַיַּֽעֲמִדֵ֖הוּ לִפְנֵ֣י פַרְעֹ֑ה וַיְבָ֥רֶךְ יַעֲקֹ֖ב אֶת־פַּרְעֹֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: After this Joseph brought in his father to the king, and presented him before him: and he blessed him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Joseph toi isänsä, Jakobin, sisälle, ja asetti hänen Pharaon eteen. Ja Jakob siunasi Pharaota.
verses.king_james: And Joseph brought in Jacob his father, and set him before Pharaoh: and Jacob blessed Pharaoh.

verses.latin_vulgate: et interrogatus ab eo : Quot sunt dies annorum vitæ tuæ ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר פַּרְעֹ֖ה אֶֽל־יַעֲקֹ֑ב כַּמָּ֕ה יְמֵ֖י שְׁנֵ֥י חַיֶּֽיךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And being asked by him: How many are the days of the years of thy life?
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Pharao sanoi Jakobille: kuinka vanha sinä olet?
verses.king_james: And Pharaoh said unto Jacob, How old art thou?

verses.latin_vulgate: respondit : Dies peregrinationis meæ centum triginta annorum sunt, parvi et mali, et non pervenerunt usque ad dies patrum meorum quibus peregrinati sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר יַעֲקֹב֙ אֶל־פַּרְעֹ֔ה יְמֵי֙ שְׁנֵ֣י מְגוּרַ֔י שְׁלֹשִׁ֥ים וּמְאַ֖ת שָׁנָ֑ה מְעַ֣ט וְרָעִ֗ים הָיוּ֙ יְמֵי֙ שְׁנֵ֣י חַיַּ֔י וְלֹ֣א הִשִּׂ֗יגוּ אֶת־יְמֵי֙ שְׁנֵי֙ חַיֵּ֣י אֲבֹתַ֔י בִּימֵ֖י מְגוּרֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: He answered: The days of my pilgrimage are a hundred and thirty years, few, and evil, and they are not come up to the days of the pilgrimage of my fathers.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jakob sanoi Pharaolle: minun kulkemiseni aika on sata ja kolmekymmentä ajastaikaa: vähä ja paha on minun elämäni aika ja ei ulotu minun isäini kulkemisen aikaan.
verses.king_james: And Jacob said unto Pharaoh, The days of the years of my pilgrimage are an hundred and thirty years: few and evil have the days of the years of my life been, and have not attained unto the days of the years of the life of my fathers in the days of their pilgrimage.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et benedicto rege, egressus est foras.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְבָ֥רֶךְ יַעֲקֹ֖ב אֶת־פַּרְעֹ֑ה וַיֵּצֵ֖א מִלִּפְנֵ֥י פַרְעֹֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And blessing the king, he went out.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jakob siunasi Pharaota, ja läksi hänen tyköänsä.
verses.king_james: And Jacob blessed Pharaoh, and went out from before Pharaoh.

verses.latin_vulgate: Joseph vero patri et fratribus suis dedit possessionem in Ægypto in optimo terræ loco, Ramesses, ut præceperat Pharao.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיּוֹשֵׁ֣ב יוֹסֵף֮ אֶת־אָבִ֣יו וְאֶת־אֶחָיו֒ וַיִּתֵּ֨ן לָהֶ֤ם אֲחֻזָּה֙ בְּאֶ֣רֶץ מִצְרַ֔יִם בְּמֵיטַ֥ב הָאָ֖רֶץ בְּאֶ֣רֶץ רַעְמְסֵ֑ס כַּאֲשֶׁ֖ר צִוָּ֥ה פַרְעֹֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Joseph gave a possession to his father and his brethren in Egypt, in the best place of the land, in Ramesses, as Pharao had commanded.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Joseph toimitti isänsä ja veljensä asumaan, ja antoi heille omaisuuden Egyptin maalla, parhaassa maan paikassa; nimittäin Rameseksen maalla, niinkuin Pharao oli käskenyt.
verses.king_james: And Joseph placed his father and his brethren, and gave them a possession in the land of Egypt, in the best of the land, in the land of Rameses, as Pharaoh had commanded.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et alebat eos, omnemque domum patris sui, præbens cibaria singulis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְכַלְכֵּ֤ל יוֹסֵף֙ אֶת־אָבִ֣יו וְאֶת־אֶחָ֔יו וְאֵ֖ת כׇּל־בֵּ֣ית אָבִ֑יו לֶ֖חֶם לְפִ֥י הַטָּֽף׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he nourished them, and all his father's house, allowing food to every one.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Joseph elätti isänsä, veljensä ja koko isänsä huoneen, sen jälkeen kuin heillä lapsia oli.
verses.king_james: And Joseph nourished his father, and his brethren, and all his father’s household, with bread, according to their families.

verses.latin_vulgate: In toto enim orbe panis deerat, et oppresserat fames terram, maxime Ægypti et Chanaan.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֶ֤חֶם אֵין֙ בְּכׇל־הָאָ֔רֶץ כִּֽי־כָבֵ֥ד הָרָעָ֖ב מְאֹ֑ד וַתֵּ֜לַהּ אֶ֤רֶץ מִצְרַ֙יִם֙ וְאֶ֣רֶץ כְּנַ֔עַן מִפְּנֵ֖י הָרָעָֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: For in the whole world there was want of bread, and a famine had oppressed the land, more especially of Egypt and Chanaan;
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja ei ollut leipää kaikessa maassa; sillä sangen raskas nälkä oli, niin että Egyptin ja Kanaanin maa nääntyi näljän tähden.
verses.king_james: And there was no bread in all the land; for the famine was very sore, so that the land of Egypt and all the land of Canaan fainted by reason of the famine.

verses.latin_vulgate: E quibus omnem pecuniam congregavit pro venditione frumenti, et intulit eam in ærarium regis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְלַקֵּ֣ט יוֹסֵ֗ף אֶת־כׇּל־הַכֶּ֙סֶף֙ הַנִּמְצָ֤א בְאֶֽרֶץ־מִצְרַ֙יִם֙ וּבְאֶ֣רֶץ כְּנַ֔עַן בַּשֶּׁ֖בֶר אֲשֶׁר־הֵ֣ם שֹׁבְרִ֑ים וַיָּבֵ֥א יוֹסֵ֛ף אֶת־הַכֶּ֖סֶף בֵּ֥יתָה פַרְעֹֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Out of which he gathered up all the money for the corn which they bought, and brought it in to the king's treasure.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Joseph kokosi kaiken rahan kuin löyttiin Egyptin ja Kanaanin maalla, jyväin edestä kuin he ostivat. Ja Joseph vei rahan Pharaon huoneesen.
verses.king_james: And Joseph gathered up all the money that was found in the land of Egypt, and in the land of Canaan, for the corn which they bought: and Joseph brought the money into Pharaoh’s house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque defecisset emptoribus pretium, venit cuncta Ægyptus ad Joseph, dicens : Da nobis panes : quare morimur coram te, deficiente pecunia ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּתֹּ֣ם הַכֶּ֗סֶף מֵאֶ֣רֶץ מִצְרַ֘יִם֮ וּמֵאֶ֣רֶץ כְּנַ֒עַן֒ וַיָּבֹ֩אוּ֩ כׇל־מִצְרַ֨יִם אֶל־יוֹסֵ֤ף לֵאמֹר֙ הָֽבָה־לָּ֣נוּ לֶ֔חֶם וְלָ֥מָּה נָמ֖וּת נֶגְדֶּ֑ךָ כִּ֥י אָפֵ֖ס כָּֽסֶף׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when the buyers wanted money, all Egypt came to Joseph, saying: Give us bread: why should we die in thy presence, having now no money?
verses.finnish_biblia: Koska raha oli kulunut Egyptin ja Kanaanin maalta, niin tulivat kaikki Egyptiläiset Josephin tykö, sanoen: anna meille leipää, miksi meidän pitää kuoleman edessäs, että raha on puuttunut?
verses.king_james: And when money failed in the land of Egypt, and in the land of Canaan, all the Egyptians came unto Joseph, and said, Give us bread: for why should we die in thy presence? for the money faileth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quibus ille respondit : Adducite pecora vestra, et dabo vobis pro eis cibos, si pretium non habetis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר יוֹסֵף֙ הָב֣וּ מִקְנֵיכֶ֔ם וְאֶתְּנָ֥ה לָכֶ֖ם בְּמִקְנֵיכֶ֑ם אִם־אָפֵ֖ס כָּֽסֶף׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he answered them: Bring me your cattle, and for them I will give you food, if you have no money.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joseph sanoi: tuokaat teidän karjanne, ja minä annan teille karjanne edestä, jos raha on puuttunut?
verses.king_james: And Joseph said, Give your cattle; and I will give you for your cattle, if money fail.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ cum adduxissent, dedit eis alimenta pro equis, et ovibus, et bobus, et asinis : sustentavitque eos illo anno pro commutatione pecorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבִ֣יאוּ אֶת־מִקְנֵיהֶם֮ אֶל־יוֹסֵף֒ וַיִּתֵּ֣ן לָהֶם֩ יוֹסֵ֨ף לֶ֜חֶם בַּסּוּסִ֗ים וּבְמִקְנֵ֥ה הַצֹּ֛אן וּבְמִקְנֵ֥ה הַבָּקָ֖ר וּבַחֲמֹרִ֑ים וַיְנַהֲלֵ֤ם בַּלֶּ֙חֶם֙ בְּכׇל־מִקְנֵהֶ֔ם בַּשָּׁנָ֖ה הַהִֽוא׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when they had brought them, he gave them food in exchange for their horses, and sheep, and oxen, and asses: and he maintained them that year for the exchange of their cattle.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja he toivat karjansa Josephille, ja Joseph antoi heille leipää hevosten, ja lammasten, ja karjan ja aasein edestä. Niin hän ruokki heitä leivällä sen vuoden, kaiken heidän karjansa edestä.
verses.king_james: And they brought their cattle unto Joseph: and Joseph gave them bread in exchange for horses, and for the flocks, and for the cattle of the herds, and for the asses: and he fed them with bread for all their cattle for that year.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venerunt quoque anno secundo, et dixerunt ei : Non celabimus dominum nostrum quod deficiente pecunia, pecora simul defecerunt : nec clam te est, quod absque corporibus et terra nihil habeamus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתִּתֹּם֮ הַשָּׁנָ֣ה הַהִוא֒ וַיָּבֹ֨אוּ אֵלָ֜יו בַּשָּׁנָ֣ה הַשֵּׁנִ֗ית וַיֹּ֤אמְרוּ לוֹ֙ לֹֽא־נְכַחֵ֣ד מֵֽאֲדֹנִ֔י כִּ֚י אִם־תַּ֣ם הַכֶּ֔סֶף וּמִקְנֵ֥ה הַבְּהֵמָ֖ה אֶל־אֲדֹנִ֑י לֹ֤א נִשְׁאַר֙ לִפְנֵ֣י אֲדֹנִ֔י בִּלְתִּ֥י אִם־גְּוִיָּתֵ֖נוּ וְאַדְמָתֵֽנוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they came the second year, and said to him: We will not hide from our lord, how that our money is spent, and our cattle also are gone: neither art thou ignorant that we have nothing now left but our bodies and our lands.
verses.finnish_biblia: Koska se vuosi oli kulunut, tulivat he hänen tykönsä toisna vuonna, ja sanoivat hänelle: emme taida salata meidän herraltamme, ettei ainoastansa raha ole kulunut, mutta myös kaikki karja on meidän herrallamme, ja ei ole mitään jäänyt meidän herramme edessä, paitsi meidän ruumistamme ja meidän peltoamme.
verses.king_james: When that year was ended, they came unto him the second year, and said unto him, We will not hide it from my lord, how that our money is spent; my lord also hath our herds of cattle; there is not ought left in the sight of my lord, but our bodies, and our lands:

verses.latin_vulgate: Cur ergo moriemur te vidente ? et nos et terra nostra tui erimus : eme nos in servitutem regiam, et præbe semina, ne pereunte cultore redigatur terra in solitudinem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָ֧מָּה נָמ֣וּת לְעֵינֶ֗יךָ גַּם־אֲנַ֙חְנוּ֙ גַּ֣ם אַדְמָתֵ֔נוּ קְנֵֽה־אֹתָ֥נוּ וְאֶת־אַדְמָתֵ֖נוּ בַּלָּ֑חֶם וְנִֽהְיֶ֞ה אֲנַ֤חְנוּ וְאַדְמָתֵ֙נוּ֙ עֲבָדִ֣ים לְפַרְעֹ֔ה וְתֶן־זֶ֗רַע וְנִֽחְיֶה֙ וְלֹ֣א נָמ֔וּת וְהָאֲדָמָ֖ה לֹ֥א תֵשָֽׁם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Why therefore shall we die before thy eyes? we will be thine, both we and our lands: buy us to be the king's servants, and give us seed, lest for want of tillers the land be turned into a wilderness.
verses.finnish_biblia: Miksi meidän pitää kuoleman edessäs ja meidän peltomme tulee kylmille? Osta meitä ja meidän maamme leivän edestä, niin me ja meidän maamme tulemme Pharaon omaksi. Anna siemeniä, että me eläisimme ja emme kuolisi, ja ei maa tulisi kylmille.
verses.king_james: Wherefore shall we die before thine eyes, both we and our land? buy us and our land for bread, and we and our land will be servants unto Pharaoh: and give us seed, that we may live, and not die, that the land be not desolate.

verses.latin_vulgate: Emit igitur Joseph omnem terram Ægypti, vendentibus singulis possessiones suas præ magnitudine famis. Subjecitque eam Pharaoni,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּ֨קֶן יוֹסֵ֜ף אֶת־כׇּל־אַדְמַ֤ת מִצְרַ֙יִם֙ לְפַרְעֹ֔ה כִּֽי־מָכְר֤וּ מִצְרַ֙יִם֙ אִ֣ישׁ שָׂדֵ֔הוּ כִּֽי־חָזַ֥ק עֲלֵהֶ֖ם הָרָעָ֑ב וַתְּהִ֥י הָאָ֖רֶץ לְפַרְעֹֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: So Joseph bought all the land of Egypt, every man selling his possessions, because of the greatness of the famine. And he brought it into Pharao's hands:
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Joseph osti koko Egyptin maan Pharaolle; sillä Egyptiläiset myivät itsekukin peltonsa, että nälkä heitä niin kovin ahdisti: ja maa tuli Pharaon omaksi.
verses.king_james: And Joseph bought all the land of Egypt for Pharaoh; for the Egyptians sold every man his field, because the famine prevailed over them: so the land became Pharaoh’s.

verses.latin_vulgate: et cunctos populos ejus a novissimis terminis Ægypti usque ad extremos fines ejus,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֶ֨ת־הָעָ֔ם הֶעֱבִ֥יר אֹת֖וֹ לֶעָרִ֑ים מִקְצֵ֥ה גְבוּל־מִצְרַ֖יִם וְעַד־קָצֵֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And all its people from one end of the borders of Egypt, even to the other end thereof,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän siirsi kansan kaupunkeihin yhdestä Egyptin äärestä niin toiseen.
verses.king_james: And as for the people, he removed them to cities from one end of the borders of Egypt even to the other end thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: præter terram sacerdotum, quæ a rege tradita fuerat eis : quibus et statuta cibaria ex horreis publicis præbebantur, et idcirco non sunt compulsi vendere possessiones suas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רַ֛ק אַדְמַ֥ת הַכֹּהֲנִ֖ים לֹ֣א קָנָ֑ה כִּי֩ חֹ֨ק לַכֹּהֲנִ֜ים מֵאֵ֣ת פַּרְעֹ֗ה וְאָֽכְל֤וּ אֶת־חֻקָּם֙ אֲשֶׁ֨ר נָתַ֤ן לָהֶם֙ פַּרְעֹ֔ה עַל־כֵּ֕ן לֹ֥א מָכְר֖וּ אֶת־אַדְמָתָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Except the land of the priests, which had been given them by the king: to whom also a certain allowance of food was given out of the public stores, and therefore they were not forced to sell their possessions.
verses.finnish_biblia: Paitsi pappein peltoa, jota ei hän ostanut: sillä se oli säätty Pharaolta, että papit söisivät sen nimitetyn osan, kuin Pharao heille antoi: sentähden ei he myyneet maatansa.
verses.king_james: Only the land of the priests bought he not; for the priests had a portion assigned them of Pharaoh, and did eat their portion which Pharaoh gave them: wherefore they sold not their lands.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit ergo Joseph ad populos : En ut cernitis, et vos et terram vestram Pharao possidet : accipite semina, et serite agros,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר יוֹסֵף֙ אֶל־הָעָ֔ם הֵן֩ קָנִ֨יתִי אֶתְכֶ֥ם הַיּ֛וֹם וְאֶת־אַדְמַתְכֶ֖ם לְפַרְעֹ֑ה הֵֽא־לָכֶ֣ם זֶ֔רַע וּזְרַעְתֶּ֖ם אֶת־הָאֲדָמָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then Joseph said to the people: Behold, as you see, both you and your lands belong to Pharao; take seed and sow the fields,
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Joseph sanoi kansalle: katso, minä olen ostanut tänäpänä teidän ja teidän maanne Pharaolle: katso, tässä on teille siemenet, kylväkäät teidän maanne.
verses.king_james: Then Joseph said unto the people, Behold, I have bought you this day and your land for Pharaoh: lo, here is seed for you, and ye shall sow the land.

verses.latin_vulgate: ut fruges habere possitis. Quintam partem regi dabitis : quatuor reliquas permitto vobis in sementem, et in cibum familiis et liberis vestris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָיָה֙ בַּתְּבוּאֹ֔ת וּנְתַתֶּ֥ם חֲמִישִׁ֖ית לְפַרְעֹ֑ה וְאַרְבַּ֣ע הַיָּדֹ֡ת יִהְיֶ֣ה לָכֶם֩ לְזֶ֨רַע הַשָּׂדֶ֧ה וּֽלְאׇכְלְכֶ֛ם וְלַאֲשֶׁ֥ר בְּבָתֵּיכֶ֖ם וְלֶאֱכֹ֥ל לְטַפְּכֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: That you may have corn. The fifth part you shall give to the king; the other four you shall have for seed, and for food for your families and children.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja teidän pitää antaman tulosta viidennen osan Pharaolle; mutta neljä osaa pitää oleman teille pellon siemeneksi, ja teille ravinnoksi, ja niille, jotka ovat teidän huoneessanne, ja ruaksi teidän lapsillenne.
verses.king_james: And it shall come to pass in the increase, that ye shall give the fifth part unto Pharaoh, and four parts shall be your own, for seed of the field, and for your food, and for them of your households, and for food for your little ones.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui responderunt : Salus nostra in manu tua est : respiciat nos tantum dominus noster, et læti serviemus regi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּאמְר֖וּ הֶחֱיִתָ֑נוּ נִמְצָא־חֵן֙ בְּעֵינֵ֣י אֲדֹנִ֔י וְהָיִ֥ינוּ עֲבָדִ֖ים לְפַרְעֹֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they answered: our life is in thy hand; only let my lord look favourably upon us, and we will gladly serve the king.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin he sanoivat: sinä olet meitä elämässä pitänyt: anna ainoastansa meidän löytää armo sinun meidän herramme edessä, ja me tahdomme olla Pharaon orjat.
verses.king_james: And they said, Thou hast saved our lives: let us find grace in the sight of my lord, and we will be Pharaoh’s servants.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ex eo tempore usque in præsentem diem, in universa terra Ægypti regibus quinta pars solvitur, et factum est quasi in legem, absque terra sacerdotali, quæ libera ab hac conditione fuit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֣שֶׂם אֹתָ֣הּ יוֹסֵ֡ף לְחֹק֩ עַד־הַיּ֨וֹם הַזֶּ֜ה עַל־אַדְמַ֥ת מִצְרַ֛יִם לְפַרְעֹ֖ה לַחֹ֑מֶשׁ רַ֞ק אַדְמַ֤ת הַכֹּֽהֲנִים֙ לְבַדָּ֔ם לֹ֥א הָיְתָ֖ה לְפַרְעֹֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: From that time unto this day, in the whole land of Egypt, the fifth part is paid to the kings, and it is become as a law, except the land of the priests, which was free from this covenant.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Joseph teki sen määrän koko Egyptin maalle, hamaan tähän päivään asti, että Pharaolle piti annettaman viides osa; paitsi pappein maata, se ei tullut Pharaon omaksi.
verses.king_james: And Joseph made it a law over the land of Egypt unto this day, that Pharaoh should have the fifth part; except the land of the priests only, which became not Pharaoh’s.

verses.latin_vulgate: Habitavit ergo Israël in Ægypto, id est, in terra Gessen, et possedit eam : auctusque est, et multiplicatus nimis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֧שֶׁב יִשְׂרָאֵ֛ל בְּאֶ֥רֶץ מִצְרַ֖יִם בְּאֶ֣רֶץ גֹּ֑שֶׁן וַיֵּאָחֲז֣וּ בָ֔הּ וַיִּפְר֥וּ וַיִּרְבּ֖וּ מְאֹֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: So Israel dwelt in Egypt, that is, in the land of Gessen, and possessed it; and grew, and was multiplied exceedingly.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Israel asui Egyptissä Gosenin maalla; ja he omistivat sen, ja kasvoivat ja suuresti lisääntyivät.
verses.king_james: And Israel dwelt in the land of Egypt, in the country of Goshen; and they had possessions therein, and grew, and multiplied exceedingly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et vixit in ea decem et septem annis : factique sunt omnes dies vitæ illius, centum quadraginta septem annorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְחִ֤י יַעֲקֹב֙ בְּאֶ֣רֶץ מִצְרַ֔יִם שְׁבַ֥ע עֶשְׂרֵ֖ה שָׁנָ֑ה וַיְהִ֤י יְמֵֽי־יַעֲקֹב֙ שְׁנֵ֣י חַיָּ֔יו שֶׁ֣בַע שָׁנִ֔ים וְאַרְבָּעִ֥ים וּמְאַ֖ת שָׁנָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he lived in it seventeen years: and all the days of his life came to a hundred and forty-seven years.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jakob eli Egyptin maalla, seitsemäntoistakymmentä ajastaikaa, ja koko hänen ikänsä oli sata ja seitsemänviidettäkymmentä ajastaikaa.
verses.king_james: And Jacob lived in the land of Egypt seventeen years: so the whole age of Jacob was an hundred forty and seven years.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque appropinquare cerneret diem mortis suæ, vocavit filium suum Joseph, et dixit ad eum : Si inveni gratiam in conspectu tuo, pone manum tuam sub femore meo : et facies mihi misericordiam et veritatem, ut non sepelias me in Ægypto :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקְרְב֣וּ יְמֵֽי־יִשְׂרָאֵל֮ לָמוּת֒ וַיִּקְרָ֣א ׀ לִבְנ֣וֹ לְיוֹסֵ֗ף וַיֹּ֤אמֶר לוֹ֙ אִם־נָ֨א מָצָ֤אתִי חֵן֙ בְּעֵינֶ֔יךָ שִֽׂים־נָ֥א יָדְךָ֖ תַּ֣חַת יְרֵכִ֑י וְעָשִׂ֤יתָ עִמָּדִי֙ חֶ֣סֶד וֶאֱמֶ֔ת אַל־נָ֥א תִקְבְּרֵ֖נִי בְּמִצְרָֽיִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when he saw that the day of his death drew nigh, he called his son Joseph, and said to him: If I have found favour in thy sight, put thy hand under my thigh; and thou shalt shew me this kindness and truth, not to bury me in Egypt.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja koska Israelin päivät joutuivat kuolla, kutsui hän poikansa Josephin, ja sanoi hänelle: jos minä olen löytänyt armon sinun edessäs, niin laske nyt kätes minun kupeeni alle, ettäs tekisit laupiuden ja totuuden minun kanssani, ettes hautaisi minua Egyptiin.
verses.king_james: And the time drew nigh that Israel must die: and he called his son Joseph, and said unto him, If now I have found grace in thy sight, put, I pray thee, thy hand under my thigh, and deal kindly and truly with me; bury me not, I pray thee, in Egypt:

verses.latin_vulgate: sed dormiam cum patribus meis, et auferas me de terra hac, condasque in sepulchro majorum meorum. Cui respondit Joseph : Ego faciam quod jussisti.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְשָֽׁכַבְתִּי֙ עִם־אֲבֹתַ֔י וּנְשָׂאתַ֙נִי֙ מִמִּצְרַ֔יִם וּקְבַרְתַּ֖נִי בִּקְבֻרָתָ֑ם וַיֹּאמַ֕ר אָנֹכִ֖י אֶֽעֱשֶׂ֥ה כִדְבָרֶֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: But I will sleep with my fathers, and thou shalt take me away out of this land, and bury me in the burying place of my ancestors. And Joseph answered him: I will do what thou hast commanded.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta minä tahdon maata minun isäini tykönä, ja sinun pitää viemän minua Egyptistä, ja hautaaman minua heidän hautaansa. Ja hän sanoi: minä teen sinun sanas jälkeen.
verses.king_james: But I will lie with my fathers, and thou shalt carry me out of Egypt, and bury me in their buryingplace. And he said, I will do as thou hast said.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ille : Jura ergo, inquit, mihi. Quo jurante, adoravit Israël Deum, conversus ad lectuli caput.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֗אמֶר הִשָּֽׁבְעָה֙ לִ֔י וַיִּשָּׁבַ֖ע ל֑וֹ וַיִּשְׁתַּ֥חוּ יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל עַל־רֹ֥אשׁ הַמִּטָּֽה׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And he said: Swear then to me. And as he was swearing, Israel adored God, turning to the bed's head.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän sanoi: vanno minulle. Ja hän vannoi hänelle. Ja Israel kallisti itsensä päänalaiselle.
verses.king_james: And he said, Swear unto me. And he sware unto him. And Israel bowed himself upon the bed’s head.
